quote_of_day.text: They fired Kairi Sane and The Wyatt Sicks among 20 to 40 other no name wrestlers from WWE. One day you are giving your life breaking your bones and jumping off from ropes and the next day you get told to GTFO. Feels bad.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of sexual paranoia moved over the board this morning, bringing with it a torrent of complaints and accusations. Anon reported feeling 'raped' by bank employees and government officials, sparking a flurry of anxious responses.

A front of social unease developed as Anon questioned India's stigmatization of sex and intimacy. This thread drew a scattered but intense response, with many weighing in on the country's attitudes towards natural human behavior.

Meanwhile, a brief but clear statement from Anon about reviving East of Indus, possibly under a new name, floated on the board like a lone cloud.

The discussion around Pajeet and Nikhil871 remained a minor but persistent drizzle in the corner of the board.

articles.title: Anon Laments 'Rape' by Bank Employees, Govt Officials
articles.body:
Anon expressed frustration on Indiachan, stating they already face enough problems and don't need to be 'raped' by government officials and bank employees.

They claimed to have issues similar to those faced by 'bladeeee,' a likely reference to a person or character.

Another anon responded with a seemingly unrelated experience, sharing that they were recently selected for a government job and found the medical process exhausting.

They described the experience at the government hospital as tiring, with many tests and examinations.

A different anon shared their own concerns about government officials and bank employees, stating they behave aggressively and that even bank managers show deference to them. This anon questioned why they have to tolerate such behavior.

One respondent offered a possible explanation for Anon's frustration, jokingly suggesting it might be because they don't create content like 'tu pyar ka sagar hai' for them.

Another anon chimed in, discussing their use of external memory aids, such as note-taking systems, to cope with information overload.

The conversation also touched on the topic of account creation on Indiachan, with one anon suggesting that creating an account would resolve issues related to posting through Tor. Another user simply approved the suggestion.

The discussion continued with various comments on memory, note-taking, and personal experiences. One user mentioned preparing for an exam and reading a book through PDF, while another discussed the benefits of externalizing information through photos rather than text.

Anon also shared their experiences with government jobs and bank interactions. One claimed that government banks are ultimately public sector undertakings and warned about the consequences of customer complaints affecting employee promotions and transfers.

articles.title: Anon questions India's stigmatization of sex and intimacy
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread by asking why sex and intimacy are stigmatized in India.

The original post read: 'why is something as natural as sex and intimacy stigmatized in this cuntree??' One anon responded by criticizing societal norms, saying that India is designed to 'castrate and infantalize' people.

This anon argued that people pretend there's nothing wrong with the current state of affairs. Another anon brought up the topic of marriage, jokingly asking when 'rakul' would get married.

A respondent expressed skepticism about their own marriage prospects, mentioning that they might end up marrying a divorcee or widow.

One participant cited statistics on age at marriage in India, noting that 62.6% of people get married before 21 years old.

Anon also discussed possible reasons for India's stigmatization of sex and intimacy, with one suggesting that it's due to religion, comparing India to other countries with similar attitudes.

Some anons made humorous and outlandish comments, including scenarios about marriage and supernatural events. A few anons shared personal anecdotes, with one admitting to having no experience with physical intimacy.

The conversation included jokes and personal attacks, with one anon telling another to stop being called 'rakul'.

articles.title: Anon Mulls Revival of East of Indus as East of Inch
articles.body:
Anon floated the idea of reviving East of Indus, a now-defunct newspaper, under a new name: East of Inch. The original poster simply stated they would give it some time and consider renaming it.

Anon expressed nostalgia for a predecessor to Indiachan, /b/, called Bhach chan, which they claimed had hundreds of lurkers, 40-60 unique posters, and around a thousand page views daily at its peak.

One respondent described it as the "worst iteration" but also the most active. Some anons discussed the potential return of activity to Indiachan following the demise of Induschan.

One suggested that with only one chan now, news might become more interesting, leading to an influx of activity.

One anon directly addressed the potential revival, stating that if enough people asked them to kill the East of Indus newspaper, they would consider it. They suspected Indiachan might see increased activity.

The conversation also touched on the history of various iterations of Indiachan. Anon mentioned Lockdown Inch as the most active, with close to 100 posters. Another expressed unironical nostalgia for Bhach chan, despite criticizing another iteration, Dyaush.

An update was provided that East of Inch is now up and running, with bi-daily news and an hourly ticker. The URL was shared for interested readers.

articles.title: Anon discusses Pajeet and Nikhil871
articles.body:
Anon posted a link to a Wikipedia page and a personal website. One anon described people like the linked individual as becoming obsolete due to AI. Another anon provided a link to a PDF file.

Anon questioned the writing style of Nikhil871, citing replacement of 's' with 'f'. A respondent explained it as '1800s English' and 'long s'.

Anon also imagined Puranavid to look like a fat neckbeard before being doxxed.
